violations.text: The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and retail area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the retail shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2024
The ceiling tiles in the retail area are in poor repair (previous water damage and holes). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
Multiple foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in and throughout the establishment. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2024
